blocks.question: Why is a Trigger Framework mandatory in Accenture projects?
blocks.answer: Accenture utilizes frameworks like fflib or custom Trigger Handlers to ensure separation of concerns and one trigger per object.

accenture.title: Accenture Salesforce Prep
accenture.subtitle: Focus on Global Delivery Model and Scalable Frameworks.

blocks.question: How do you handle Large Data Volumes (LDV) in a Deloitte global org?
blocks.answer: Handling LDV requires a multi-layered approach: Skinny Tables, Custom Indexes, and Divisons.

deloitte.title: Deloitte Salesforce Interview (2026)
deloitte.subtitle: Advanced architectural screening and scenario drills for Senior Roles.

blocks.question: Layer 1: Organization Level Security?
blocks.answer: Login IP Ranges, Login Hours, Password Policies, and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).

blocks.question: Layer 2: Object Level Security?
blocks.answer: Profiles and Permission Sets control CRED (Create, Read, Edit, Delete) permissions.

blocks.question: Layer 3: Field Level Security?
blocks.answer: Profiles and Permission Sets control visibility and editability of specific fields.

blocks.question: Layer 4: Record Level Security?
blocks.answer: OWD (Organization Wide Defaults), Role Hierarchy, Sharing Rules, and Manual Sharing.

blocks.question: Layer 5: Folder Level Security?
blocks.answer: Visibility of Reports, Dashboards, and Email Templates.

security_5_layers.title: Salesforce 5 Layers of Security
security_5_layers.subtitle: Complete breakdown of the Salesforce Security Model.

blocks.question: What happens before triggers run?
blocks.answer: Salesforce loads the original record and performs initial system validation (field formats, length).

blocks.question: Explain the 20-step execution order.
blocks.answer: 1. System Validation, 2. Before Triggers, 3. Custom Validation, 4. Duplicate Rules, 5. Save (no commit), 6. After Triggers, 7. Assignment Rules, 8. Auto-response, 9. Workflow Rules, 10. Process Builder/Flows, 11. Escalation Rules, 12. Roll-up Summaries, 13. Re-save (if needed).

order_of_execution.title: Order of Execution
order_of_execution.subtitle: Master the 20+ steps of the Salesforce Save Cycle.

blocks.question: What is the difference between Entitlements and Service Contracts?
blocks.answer: Entitlements are the support terms (e.g. 24/7 phone support), while Service Contracts represent the commercial agreement.

blocks.question: How does Omni-Channel routing work?
blocks.answer: Omni-Channel uses Service Channels and Routing Configurations to push work items to agents based on capacity and skill.

service_cloud.title: Service Cloud Architecture
service_cloud.subtitle: Advanced Service Console and Case Management strategies.

blocks.question: Explain Guest User Security in Experience Cloud.
blocks.answer: Guest users cannot own records and must have 'Secure guest user record access' enabled, using Sharing Sets/Criteria for visibility.

blocks.question: What are Opportunity Splits?
blocks.answer: Splits allow multiple team members to share credit for an opportunity based on their contribution (Revenue vs Overlay).

sales_cloud.title: Sales Cloud Architecture
sales_cloud.subtitle: Lead-to-Cash and Pipeline Management deep dive.

blocks.question: When should you use a Screen Flow vs a Record-Triggered Flow?
blocks.answer: Screen Flows are for guided user interactions; Record-Triggered Flows are for background updates on DML events.

flow_master.title: Flow Master Class
flow_master.subtitle: Industrial automation patterns and fault handling.

blocks.question: What are the 5 core components of Agentforce?
blocks.answer: 1. Agent, 2. Topics, 3. Actions, 4. Atlas, 5. Trust Layer.
